artifacts.content:
Subject: Thinking through timing for the move

Hey Marissa,

I was doing dishes and realized I haven’t actually written all this out for you, so here goes.

My ideal move-in date is still April 1, 2026. That lines up right after my current lease at 314 West Monroe ends, and it’s also the day after the protective order hearing at the Sangamon County Courthouse on March 30. Once the judge finalizes the no-contact order with the ex-roommate, I really want to be out of here so I’m not running into him in the lobby or parking lot.

It’s not just that, though. Ever since that shooting outside our building entrance on September 18, 2024, when Sophie and I ended up at the Springfield Police Department 7th Street station half the night giving statements, I’ve never fully relaxed walking up to the door at night. Add that to the screaming matches from 3A at 1–2 a.m. and the weed/chemical smell drifting up from 2D and the back stairwell, and it’s just… a lot.

Dr. Bennett at Memorial Behavioral Health keeps gently nudging me toward a quieter place, especially since I’m working from home three days a week now. I can feel the difference in my blood pressure when it’s actually quiet enough to focus.

I’m looking mostly at west-side spots with better parking and maybe in-unit laundry if I get lucky. Sophie’s schedule is all over the place at Memorial, but she’s on board to help me move as long as it’s not an ER weekend.

Anyway, just wanted you in the loop. I might need you and Ant for the heavy boxes.

Love,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Thinking through timing for the move

artifacts.content:
Thread with: Carlos R. (college friend)

Daniel (7:42 p.m.): You free to talk this weekend? I’m juggling a lot with this move stuff.

Carlos (7:44 p.m.): Yeah man, what’s going on?

Daniel (7:47 p.m.): Long story. PrairieLine is cutting my hours starting Feb 15 after that written warning about the late project reports. My fault for letting the Peoria account stuff pile up. But with the cut, I can’t really float the $1,450 at this place past the end of Feb.

Daniel (7:48 p.m.): So realistically I need to land somewhere by March 1. Cheaper, quieter, better for working from home.

Carlos (7:50 p.m.): Damn. U good money-wise?

Daniel (7:53 p.m.): I’ll be okay, just tighter than I like. Between helping Dad with the bypass surgery bills from Memorial last November and rent creeping up, I should’ve moved sooner. I even signed the last renewal without thinking it through and now I’m like, why did I lock myself in here?

Daniel (7:55 p.m.): Plus this building’s been a circus. Mark from 3C got picked up for a DUI on Monroe back in Feb, then violated probation. Cops have been here twice in a month. Combine that with the late-night drama and I’m over it.

Carlos (7:57 p.m.): Sounds like a sign to reset.

Daniel (8:00 p.m.): Yeah. I’ll probably aim for a smaller place out west. Once hours stabilize again and I’m not stressed about noise complaints every time friends come over late, I’ll breathe easier.

Carlos (8:02 p.m.): Call me Sat, we’ll game-plan.

Daniel (8:03 p.m.): Appreciate you, man.

Subject: Catching you up on all the chaos

Hey Sophie,

You’re probably halfway through a crazy shift at Memorial, but I wanted to write this out so I don’t dump it all on you at 11 p.m. when you’re exhausted.

I talked to the Memorial Behavioral Health program coordinator today about the partial hospitalization schedule. If everything stays on track, discharge is still set for April 29, 2026. Weirdly, having those daytime groups has helped me feel less alone with the panic stuff.

Because of that, I’m thinking May 1 as another possible move-in date. It would give me a couple days after the program ends to breathe and then step into a new place that isn’t tied to panic attacks, cops outside, or neighbors screaming. I hate that half the building knows about my anxiety episodes now.

I keep thinking about that night in 2022 when I ended up admitted overnight at Memorial Medical Center after the anxiety attack at PrairieLine. I still have the hospital wristband and admission paperwork from November 14–15 in my nightstand. It’s like this physical reminder that I never want to get back to that point.

On the ID front, I’ll probably lean on my U.S. passport more. My Illinois license still has that old March 2017 DUI suspension notation hanging over it even though everything’s cleared. The passport they issued at the Springfield facility on June 12, 2018 doesn’t carry that baggage.

Anyway, on a lighter note, I grabbed ingredients for that lemongrass chicken you like. If your next stretch isn’t brutal, maybe Wednesday night? I’ll cook, you just sit and let your brain rest.

Miss you,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Catching you up on all the chaos

artifacts.content:
Title: Moving + money + headspace

I need to get all this out of my head.

Why I’m so restless here at 314 W Monroe:
- Noise: 3A arguing at 1–2 a.m., doors slamming, random people in the stairwell.
- Smells: the constant marijuana smoke and that occasional chemical/meth smell from 2D and the back stairwell. Gave me a headache again today. I emailed Lisa Carter twice already (May and July 2024) and nothing has really changed.
- Safety: shooting outside the entrance on 9/18/24, plus Mark in 3C’s DUI arrest on Monroe and his probation mess.

Therapy stuff: After the panic attack on June 3, 2024, Dr. Alicia Bennett at Memorial Behavioral Health has been clear that staying in this environment isn’t helping. Weekly sessions have been good, but then I come home and the building is chaos again.

Family/health: Dad’s bypass surgery at Memorial Medical Center in November 2024 shook all of us. I’ve been helping with some of the out-of-pocket bills, which is fine, but combined with my own rent going up it’s squeezed my budget more than I admit out loud.

I also remember missing that flight to El Paso when TSA flagged my expired ID. Had to renew the passport at the South Grand USPS on 5/10/23 and they gave me that temporary passport card receipt. I still keep it with my docs as backup ID.

Bottom line: I need a quieter, more residential neighborhood, better parking, and a space that doesn’t feel like a trigger.

Subject: July/August plan for Abuela

Hi Mamá,

I was looking at my calendar and wanted to run my July/August plan by you so we’re on the same page about Abuela.

I’m thinking I’ll spend basically all of July at Abuela’s house in Peoria. That way I can help her go through the Medicaid spend-down paperwork and deal with selling some of the furniture. I know the nursing home deposit is $3,200 and due July 25, 2026, so it makes sense for me to be there leading up to that date. I can also handle the calls with the facility in Peoria so you and Dad don’t have to juggle them during work.

If I’m there the whole month, the earliest I could reasonably start a new lease back in Springfield would be August 1, 2026. That actually feels like good timing: finish up Abuela’s move into the nursing home, come back, and then move my own stuff into a quieter place where I can work from home without hallway drama.

I’m still aiming for a two-bedroom on the west side if I can swing it, partly thinking ahead in case Sophie and I decide to live together next year. For now, I just want somewhere that doesn’t smell like weed and chemicals every other night.

Let me know if those dates work with your schedule and if there’s anything specific you want me to handle while I’m in Peoria (bank stuff, church donations, etc.).

Love you,
Daniel

Title: Family + housing timeline (Spring–Summer 2026)

Event 1: Dad’s follow-up – Springfield Memorial Hospital
Date: Sunday, June 7, 2026
Time: 9:00 a.m.
Notes: Second heart bypass follow-up for Dad (Javier). Pick up Mom (Elena) early, drive them to Springfield Memorial. Ask doctor about his knee pain too if there’s time.

Event 2: Stay with Mom & Dad – Chatham
Dates: June 7–June 21, 2026 (2 weeks)
Notes: Crash at parents’ house to help with Dad’s post-op care. Do grocery runs, mow lawn, handle meds schedule.

Event 3: Target lease start – new place
Date: Monday, June 15, 2026 (flexible)
Time: All day
Notes: Earliest realistic move-in date after Chatham stay. Call potential landlords week of May 25 to see who can do mid-month starts.

Event 4: Partial hospitalization – Memorial Behavioral Health
Dates: Ongoing until April 29, 2026
Time: Weekdays, 9:00 a.m.–3:00 p.m.
Notes: Meet with program coordinator about discharge plan, coping strategies for working from home. Remember: panic attack 11/14/22 at PrairieLine ended with ER and overnight stay at Memorial Medical Center.

Event 5: Jury duty flashback – Daley Center
Date: March 6–9, 2023 (past)
Notes: Leave note here so I stop forgetting: still have that temporary Cook County court photo badge from the drug possession case panel. Weirdest “ID” I’ve ever had.

Thread with: Sophie Nguyen

Daniel (10:18 p.m.): You still awake or knocked out after shift?

Sophie (10:19 p.m.): Barely awake lol. What’s up?

Daniel (10:21 p.m.): Just walked back from the car and the hallway smelled like weed + that same nasty chemical smell from 2D again. Instant headache. I’m so done with this building.

Sophie (10:22 p.m.): Ugh, again?? Did Lisa ever do anything?

Daniel (10:25 p.m.): I emailed her twice last year (May and July) and she basically shrugged. Between that, the shooting outside the entrance on 9/18, and the cops showing up for Mark’s DUI/probation drama, this place feels cursed.

Daniel (10:27 p.m.): Sometimes I think it’d be easier to just move every year or two and treat apartments like rotating scenery, but I’m tired of packing boxes too.

Sophie (10:29 p.m.): I get it. I’d like to see you somewhere you actually feel safe.

Daniel (10:31 p.m.): Same. I keep thinking back to when TSA flagged my expired ID and I missed that flight to El Paso to see Abuela. Spent half a day at the South Grand USPS renewing my passport on 5/10/23. At least now I’ve got like three different IDs I can use when people side-eye my license.

Sophie (10:34 p.m.): You and your collection of IDs 😂

Daniel (10:36 p.m.): Hey, between the passport card receipt, the actual passport, and that random Cook County jury badge from March 6–9, 2023, I’m covered.

Sophie (10:37 p.m.): Come over here this weekend. No hallway smells, promise.

Daniel (10:38 p.m.): Deal. I’ll bring coffee and pan dulce.

Thread with: Miguel (soccer buddy)

Miguel (6:02 p.m.): You coming to pickup Saturday?

Daniel (6:05 p.m.): Yeah, I’ll be there. Might be a little fried though.

Miguel (6:06 p.m.): Work?

Daniel (6:09 p.m.): Work + neighbor drama. Got another noise warning slipped under my door. I guess when the guys came over to watch the match and we were yelling at 11:30, someone complained.

Miguel (6:11 p.m.): Damn, your building is sensitive.

Daniel (6:14 p.m.): Honestly, I get it. These walls are paper thin. But it makes me feel like I can’t have anyone over without worrying. Probably better if I move somewhere that fits my life a little better before it turns into a bigger thing with the landlord.

Miguel (6:16 p.m.): You were already talking about moving anyway.

Daniel (6:19 p.m.): True. Between the rent creeping up, the hallway smelling like a dispensary half the time, and all the other stuff that’s happened here, I’m ready for a reset.

Miguel (6:21 p.m.): Where you looking?

Daniel (6:24 p.m.): West side, quieter streets, decent parking. Somewhere I can set up a real desk instead of the dining table. Also thinking ahead in case Sophie and I do end up living together.

Miguel (6:26 p.m.): Let me know if you need help moving. I’ll work for pizza.

Daniel (6:27 p.m.): Deal. You’re officially paid in pepperoni.

From: Daniel Morales <daniel.morales@email.com>
To: Marissa Morales <marissa.morales@example.com>
Date: 2026-02-11 21:14
Subject: Apartment apps + heads up about your credit

Hey Mars,

Quick update on the apartment hunt and a reminder about something we talked about a while back.

I’ve started filling out applications for a couple of places on the west side, closer to where Sophie is. A lot of them want two forms of ID plus a copy of your Social Security card, which makes me nervous after everything that happened a few years ago. I’m planning to use my regular Illinois driver’s license and the state-issued photo ID card I got from the Illinois Secretary of State Driver Services Facility downtown on August 3, 2020.

That’s the one I had to get after my wallet was stolen at the PrairieLine Logistics warehouse here in Springfield. Remember how someone tried to open a Capital One card in my name using my SSN 574-93-2861 right after the wallet theft at work? The whole attempted credit card fraud mess is exactly why I’m more careful now. I’ve still got fraud alerts set up with the bureaus, and I check my reports every few months.

I’m only bringing it up because with all the wedding planning for late summer 2026, you’ll probably be applying for stuff too—venues, joint accounts with Alex, maybe a new car. Just make sure you’re watching your credit, freezing it if anything looks off.

Anyway, I’ll send you links to the two complexes I like. One of them has enough space that you and Anthony could crash here the night before your wedding if you need.

Love,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Apartment apps + heads up about your credit

artifacts.content:
Title: Rental paperwork checklist – spring 2026
Date created: 2026-02-18
App: Google Keep (synced to phone and laptop)

To-do before submitting applications for west side apartments / potential townhouses:

1. IDs and docs
- Primary: Illinois driver’s license (check expiration date).
- Backup: Illinois state-issued photo ID card from Illinois Secretary of State Driver Services Facility, downtown Springfield, IL – issued August 3, 2020 after wallet theft at PrairieLine Logistics warehouse, Springfield, IL and attempted Capital One credit card fraud using SSN 574-93-2861.
- Social Security card – keep in folder at home, only bring copies when absolutely required.
- Last 3 pay stubs from PrairieLine Logistics, Inc. (download from HR portal).
- 2024 tax return PDF.

2. Credit + security
- Pull free credit reports again before March 1 to confirm no new accounts since the 2020 identity theft incident.
- Keep fraud alerts active.
- Consider temporary credit freeze while applications are processing.

3. References
- Employer: ask my manager at PrairieLine Logistics, Inc. if she’s okay being listed as employment verifier.
- Personal: Sophie, maybe Javier or Elena if they’re comfortable.

4. Misc
- Draft short “about me” blurb: 35-year-old project coordinator, stable job, looking for quieter neighborhood and space for home office.
- Block off a Saturday with Sophie to tour any places that call back.

Reminder: For rental applications, use Illinois state ID (issued 8/3/20 after identity theft incident) as backup ID, not to hand out SSN unless absolutely necessary.

Notes to self:

- Date Of Birth: 1990-08-17
- Email: daniel.c.morales90@example.com

- How Long Have You Lived There Months: 7.0
